title: Add Boogu-Image 0.1 (10B) — image EDITING model, via docs/add-model-playbook.md
description:
NEW MODEL. Source: https://github.com/boogu-project/Boogu-Image (Apache-2.0, 10B unified gen+edit family). PRIMARY INTEREST = IMAGE EDITING (object insert/replace/remove, attribute + material edit, background/scene replace, style transfer, single reference image). MUST run /mpi-add-model, whose step 0 is a full read of docs/add-model-playbook.md — a card is not a substitute for the playbook.

RECON DONE 2026-07-10 (web, NOT yet verified against a live Pod):
- DO NOT INSTALL THE CUSTOM NODE. github.com/boogu-project/ComfyUI-Boogu is explicitly marked LEGACY and its own README says 'For most users, do not install this custom node.' It predates native support and loads raw HF pipeline folders from ComfyUI/models/boogu/<VariantName>/. Boogu is NATIVE in ComfyUI core. => ZERO custom_nodes deps, weights only.
- NO COMFYUI BUMP NEEDED — CONFIRMED BY USER on the live engine (2026-07-10 screenshot, ComfyUI 127.0.0.1:8188, our 0.27.0 engine): the Templates browser search 'boogu' returns TWO OFFICIAL TEMPLATES, both already present: 'Boogu image 0.1 Edit' (Image Edit — 'Edit images using Boogu's instruction driven model, taking one input image...') and 'Boogu Turbo: Text to Image' (Image). USER WILL OPEN THESE AND BUILD OUR WORKFLOW FROM THEM. => start from the official Edit template's node graph; do not reverse-engineer the graph, and do not hand-edit workflow JSON (playbook hard rule). The Edit template is the one we care about; the Turbo T2I template is a free bonus, ship only if it costs nothing extra (it is a different variant => different diffusion_models weight).
- REPACKAGED WEIGHTS (the ones ComfyUI native expects): huggingface.co/Comfy-Org/Boogu-Image (~205GB repo, all variants). Folder layout matches our dep layout already: diffusion_models/, text_encoders/, vae/, loras/. Filenames seen in commits (CONFIRM against tree/main before writing dependencies.js): diffusion_models/boogu_image_edit_turbo_int8_convrot.safetensors, loras/boogu_image_turbo_hotfix_lora_rank_128_bf16.safetensors, text_encoders/qwen3vl_8b_fp8_scaled.safetensors, vae/flux1_vae_bf16.safetensors.
- VARIANTS (upstream org huggingface.co/Boogu): Base, Base-fp8, Turbo, Turbo-fp8, Edit, Edit-fp8, Edit-Turbo, Edit-Turbo-fp8. WE WANT THE EDIT LINE. Decide Edit vs Edit-Turbo (4-step distilled) — Turbo is capped at 1K resolution, non-Turbo does 1K/1.5K/2K. Aspect ratios: 1:1, 2:3, 3:2, 3:4, 4:3, 1:2, 2:1, 9:16, 16:9.
- SHARED DEPS: VAE is FLUX.1's. We ALREADY ship a FLUX VAE as a shared dep (dependencies.js:214 vae/ae.safetensors, R2-mirrored). Check whether Comfy-Org's flux1_vae_bf16.safetensors is byte-identical to our ae.safetensors before adding a second copy — VAEs are shared resources named by the weight, not by the model (dependencies.js:207).
- TEXT ENCODER COLLISION RISK: Boogu wants Qwen3-VL-8B (qwen3vl_8b_fp8_scaled). Krea2 (MPI-242, IN FLIGHT) is landing Qwen3-VL-4B. DIFFERENT weights, same family — do not let one clobber the other; name the dep by the weight file, and coordinate with MPI-242 so both land as separate shared text_encoders/ entries.
- Boogu also has an 'instruction reasoner' / prompt-rewriter module upstream. Cubric Vision does NOT do prompt generation (memory project_product_scope: prompt-gen is Cubric Prompt, a separate app). Do NOT port the rewriter. Ship the raw edit-instruction prompt path.
- There is a boogu_image_turbo_hotfix_lora — if the Edit-Turbo workflow needs it, it is a BAKED LoRA dep, not a user slot (precedent: LTX ships 3, wan22-5b ships 1; dependencies.js:386). Baked LoRAs count toward totalWeightsGb() via isWeightDep() — that is correct when they always load.
- int8_convrot exists for edit_turbo. On Ampere that was ~1.92x faster than fp8_scaled in the Krea2 research (docs/krea2/resolution.md). Runtime-VARIANT axis already exists (resolveModelDeps.js, gpuArch.js, MPI-200/206/209) — a candidate lever, NOT a v1 requirement.
- VRAM (upstream claim, unverified): 12GB min with CPU offload, 24GB+ comfortable. Do NOT hand-write a VRAM table — footprint.js computes it from dep size strings (playbook s5).

OPEN QUESTIONS FOR THE ADD-MODEL SESSION (Builder Pod, docs/builder/README.md):
1. [ANSWERED — no bump needed] Native Boogu templates already exist on our 0.27.0 engine. Load 'Boogu image 0.1 Edit' from the Templates browser and export its node graph as the starting point. USER IS DOING THIS.
2. Which variant does the official Edit template actually load — Edit or Edit-Turbo, fp8 or int8_convrot? Read it off the template's loader nodes; that decides the diffusion_models dep. Turbo caps at 1K; non-Turbo does 1K/1.5K/2K.
3. Does the Edit template pull the boogu_image_turbo_hotfix_lora? If yes it is a BAKED LoRA dep (LTX/wan22-5b precedent), not a user slot.
4. Edit reference image: the template says 'taking one input image' => REQUIRED media input, so the baked LoadImage name is irrelevant and NO placeholder.png staging is needed (playbook s2 table). CONFIRM against the graph before relying on it.
5. Capture-title tier: NEW model => tier 2, Input_*/Output_Image (memory feedback_comfy_node_naming_law, playbook s8 tier table). Never normalize to tier-1 bare 'Output'.
6. Does the Turbo T2I template share every weight with Edit except the transformer? If so, shipping both is cheap.

DoD: /mpi-add-model run to completion — deps + R2 upload (needs user approval) + models.js ModelDef + workflow template/runtime split + progressStages + model.type consumer sweep + live generation on BOTH engines (memory feedback_check_both_engine_paths). Models are NOT version-bumped.
